meta.name: JWT Attacks
meta.description: JSON Web Token attack payloads and techniques. Covers algorithm confusion (alg:none, RS→HS), weak secret exploitation, kid injection, header parameter abuse, and claim tampering.

payloads.description: alg:none JWT with admin role — base64(header)+'.'+base64(payload)+'.' (empty sig). Header: {alg:none}
JWT header with alg:none — disables signature verification in vulnerable libraries
Uppercase NONE variant — some parsers are case-sensitive in their alg:none check
Mixed-case nOnE — evades case-sensitive alg:none blacklist
Switch RS256→HS256 — use server's public key as HMAC secret for signature confusion attack
Python HS256 signed with RSA public key — exploits RS256→HS256 algorithm confusion
ECDSA ES256 variant for algorithm confusion — rare but exists in some libraries
Brute-force HS256 JWT secret with hashcat — mode 16500 is jwt
JohnTheRipper JWT crack — alternative to hashcat for HS256 secrets

payloads.code:
secret
secret123
yoursecret
changeme
jwtsecret
supersecret
payloads.description: Common JWT weak secrets wordlist — try these first before running rockyou
kid path traversal to /dev/null — signs JWT with empty string key (null device returns nothing)
kid traversal to predictable file — sign with known file content as HMAC key
kid SQL injection — if kid is used in a DB lookup, inject custom key value
kid command injection — if kid is used in shell command, inject custom secret
JWK header injection — embeds attacker's own public key in header for self-signed JWT
jku header injection — points to attacker-hosted JWKS file for key verification
x5u header injection — points to attacker certificate for X.509 chain validation bypass
Claim tampering — escalate role to superadmin and extend expiry
Admin flag injection — add admin:true claim with far-future expiry
Path traversal in sub claim — exploits systems using sub as file path or DB key
